hero.headline: The headless engine for your next SaaS.
hero.subheadline: Auth, payments, subscriptions, credits, RBAC, CMS, and i18n — all pre-wired. Just build your product on top.

features.title: Everything you need to ship
features.description: Pre-wired business logic so you can focus on building your product.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: Email/password, Google, and GitHub OAuth — ready out of the box with session management and account linking.

payment.title: Payments
payment.description: Stripe, PayPal, Alipay, WeChat Pay — checkout, subscriptions, webhooks, and order management.

rbac.title: Roles & Permissions
rbac.description: Fine-grained RBAC with wildcard matching. Full admin panel for managing users, roles, and permissions.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: English and Chinese built-in with locale-aware routing. Easy to extend with additional languages.

cms.title: Content Management
cms.description: Categories and posts with full CRUD, status management, and admin panel — build your blog or docs.

credits.title: Credit System
credits.description: FIFO consumption, expiration, auto-grant on signup, and revocation — ready for usage-based billing.

pricing.title: Simple pricing
pricing.description: Choose the plan that works for you.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know before getting started.

stack.question: What's the tech stack?
stack.answer: Next.js 16 with the App Router, React 19, TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4, shadcn/ui, Drizzle ORM, and better-auth. Every piece of business logic is inlined — no external packages for payment, email, storage, or AI.

payment.question: Which payment providers are supported?
payment.answer: Stripe, PayPal, Creem, Alipay, and WeChat Pay out of the box. The PaymentManager abstraction makes it straightforward to add more providers by implementing a single interface.

database.question: Which databases can I use?
database.answer: Drizzle ORM handles PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Turso, and Cloudflare D1. Switch providers by editing DATABASE_PROVIDER in your .env — the schema templates and drizzle config adapt automatically.

customize.question: How customizable is the landing page?
customize.answer: The src/blocks/ directory ships with demo sections — they're designed to be rewritten per project. The durable primitives live in src/components/, so your rebrand touches blocks and translations, not the chassis.

license.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
license.answer: Yes. ShipAny Next is built for shipping production SaaS products. You own the code after cloning — no per-seat fees, no runtime dependencies on our infrastructure.

cta.headline: Ready to ship your SaaS?
cta.subheadline: Stop wiring auth, payments, and RBAC from scratch. Start with ShipAny Next and build what actually makes your product unique.
